# /knowledge-dream — Manual local consolidator run

Runs the same consolidation logic as the GitHub workflows, but locally and
against the local mirror. Useful during bootstrap when the wiki is small and
you want fast feedback, or when previewing what the next nightly dream will do.

## When to invoke

- During Phase 0–3 bootstrap when iterating on proposed/ → wiki/ promotion.
- When you've just written a lot of journal entries and want to see them
  consolidated without waiting for the workflow.
- To preview-debug the consolidation prompt itself before pushing changes
  to `dream-light.yml` / `dream.yml`.

DO NOT invoke as part of a normal coding session — the GitHub workflows will
run the dream within minutes of your push, and they coordinate via the
`concurrency: dream` group so they don't race.

## How

```bash
cd ~/.leadbay/knowledge
git pull --rebase --autostash
# Light pass — only entries since last dream
LB_LANE=cold python3 bin/lb-knowledge-validate-all
# (For the actual consolidation prompt, follow the steps in
# .github/workflows/dream-light.yml or dream.yml against the journal/proposed
# entries that need processing. Local invocation is documented in
# leadbay/knowledge/CLAUDE.md.)
```

## What you must follow

The strict write rules from `CLAUDE.md` in `leadbay/knowledge`:
- Cold lane (this skill operating with `LB_LANE=cold`) is the ONLY lane allowed
  to write `wiki/`, `glossary.md`, `index.md`, `log.md`.
- Hot lane (every other agent invocation) writes only to `journal/`,
  `proposed/`, `sources/`, `questions/`.
- Provenance is mandatory on every wiki page.
- Promote proposals only with cross-source quorum (≥ 2 distinct slugs) or
  explicit `force_promote: true`.

When done locally, `git push` from `~/.leadbay/knowledge` so the workflows
don't redo the work.
